channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Putin says Russia will bolster air defenses in response to Ukrainian drone attacks
item.description: President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.
item.content:encoded:
President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.

(Image credit: Dmitri Lovetsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: North Korea unveils a new plant to produce fuel for nuclear weapons
item.description: State media photos on the place showed it is likely a plant to produce weapons-grade uranium.
item.content:encoded:
State media photos on the place showed it is likely a plant to produce weapons-grade uranium.

(Image credit: Korean Central News Agency)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hezbollah rejects ceasefire deal agreed on by Israel and Lebanon
item.description: The U.N. peacekeeping mission for Lebanon, known as UNIFIL, said one peacekeeper was killed and others were wounded when they came under mortar fire in southeastern Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
The U.N. peacekeeping mission for Lebanon, known as UNIFIL, said one peacekeeper was killed and others were wounded when they came under mortar fire in southeastern Lebanon.

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Russia's economic forum opens against backdrop of Ukrainian strikes
item.description: The St. Petersburg International Economic Forum -- Russia's answer to Davos -- opens under the shadow of Ukrainian drone attacks and growing economic uncertainty.
item.content:encoded: The St. Petersburg International Economic Forum -- Russia's answer to Davos -- opens under the shadow of Ukrainian drone attacks and growing economic uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Peru election could add to string of right-wing victories in Latin America
item.description: Peruvians go to the polls Sunday to elect their next president amid a conservative shift in Latin America. The daughter of a former dictator, a far-right senator, could win.
item.content:encoded: Peruvians go to the polls Sunday to elect their next president amid a conservative shift in Latin America. The daughter of a former dictator, a far-right senator, could win.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Plans for a Trump family-linked resort spark protests in Albania
item.description: A coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner is facing resistance in Albania. The government says the project will transform the nation, but environmental campaigners and critics oppose it.
item.content:encoded:
A coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner is facing resistance in Albania. The government says the project will transform the nation, but environmental campaigners and critics oppose it.

(Image credit: Hameraldi Agolli)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Video of a dying teen handcuffed by police sparks outrage and riots across Britain
item.description: There is outrage in the U.K. over the murder of 18-year-old Henry Nowak. Bodycam footage shows police handcuffing him as he lay dying. His killer falsely accused Nowak of racist abuse.
item.content:encoded:
There is outrage in the U.K. over the murder of 18-year-old Henry Nowak. Bodycam footage shows police handcuffing him as he lay dying. His killer falsely accused Nowak of racist abuse.

(Image credit: Justin Tallis)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy,' says they still get along
item.description: The president also acknowledged that he cursed at the Israeli leader in a heated phone call in which he told Benjamin Netanyahu not to bomb the Lebanese capital Beirut.
item.content:encoded:
The president also acknowledged that he cursed at the Israeli leader in a heated phone call in which he told Benjamin Netanyahu not to bomb the Lebanese capital Beirut.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Greetings from Porto, whose lanes are lined with colorful textiles
item.description: Linen scarves, cotton aprons and dishtowels adorn the entrances to souvenir shops, many of which are run by Bangladeshis whose home country shares Portugal's rich tradition of textile manufacturing.
item.content:encoded: Linen scarves, cotton aprons and dishtowels adorn the entrances to souvenir shops, many of which are run by Bangladeshis whose home country shares Portugal's rich tradition of textile manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Esme Nicholson

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike a St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin visit
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as the city hosted an annual economic forum promoted by President Putin.
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as the city hosted an annual economic forum promoted by President Putin.

(Image credit: AP Photo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
